assertion_results.text: The changelog identifies this as a patch version bump (v4.2.1)
assertion_results.evidence: Header: '## [4.2.1] — Security Patch' with note 'Patch release addressing a high-severity security vulnerability.'

assertion_results.text: The security vulnerability is described with advisory language including severity level (HIGH) and CVSS score (8.1)
Affected versions (4.0.0 - 4.2.0) are explicitly stated so operators know if they need to upgrade
The changelog communicates upgrade urgency — words like 'urgent', 'immediately', 'critical', or 'recommended to upgrade' appear
assertion_results.evidence: Opening paragraph: 'All users on versions 4.0.0 through 4.2.0 are **strongly recommended to upgrade immediately**.'

assertion_results.text: The open redirect vulnerability description explains the attack vector (URL-encoded characters bypassing validation)
assertion_results.evidence: Description: 'An attacker could craft a malicious redirect_uri using URL-encoded characters to bypass validation, redirecting users to attacker-controlled sites.'

assertion_results.text: The CSRF fix (state parameter check) is documented as a separate entry from the redirect fix
assertion_results.evidence: Two separate entries under ### Bug Fixes: redirect validation fix (#890) and state parameter CSRF check (#891)

assertion_results.text: A compare link is included for the diff between v4.2.0 and v4.2.1
The changelog does NOT include the test commit as a user-facing change (tests are internal)
assertion_results.evidence: The test commit 'add regression tests for redirect validation (#892)' still appears under an 'Internal' section.
